$comment: Pinned, SHA-verified esbuild prebuilt channel for the M3 TypeScript toolchain (src/runtime/ts/, issue #83 / L-61 / R-LANG-002/004). SINGLE source of truth for the pins — parsed by tools/fetch_esbuild.py (download + verify-before-use + extract the standalone binary). src/runtime/ts/CMakeLists.txt forwards this file's PATH to fetch_esbuild.py (it does NOT parse the pins itself, so they are never duplicated) and resolves the extracted binary name (esbuild / esbuild.exe) by the platform naming convention. Channel = esbuild's OWN per-platform npm packages (@esbuild/<platform>), the canonical publisher artifacts — the SAME SHA-pinned third-party build-tool precedent as tools/v8-prebuilt.json / wgpu-native / CEF codified in docs/signing.md: third-party build tools are authenticated by their OWN publisher via TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, and are explicitly OUT of scope for the engine R-SEC-009 first-party trust root (do NOT route these through tools/verify_artifact.py). esbuild is a standalone native Go binary (a build-TIME transpiler/bundler; NOT linked into the shipped engine — unlike rusty_v8), so unlike the V8 prebuilt it builds/runs on EVERY toolchain including the local Ninja+Strawberry-GCC Windows dev gate (it is invoked as a subprocess, never linked). VERSION-COHERENCE (every per-platform package carries the SAME esbuild_version) is the critical correctness invariant — fetch_esbuild.py asserts it at runtime (fail-closed) and tools/tests/test_fetch_esbuild.py asserts it statically. A tsc-class SEMANTIC typecheck (--noEmit, ts.type_error surfacing) + derivation-node compile caching (R-FILE-010) are the tracked follow-up (see src/runtime/ts/README.md § Deferred seams); esbuild deliberately does NOT typecheck, it transpiles+bundles.
